name: Cylindrical Coordinates (Introduction)
description: Extending polar to 3D: polar in the base, plus height above/below.

content.overview: Think of a cylinder (like a soda can). To describe a point in or on it, you use polar for the circular part (distance and angle) plus height (z). That's cylindrical! {{viz:diagram-01}}
keyPoints: Perfect for anything with circular symmetry
content.whyItMatters: Rotating systems, vortexes, turbines—all are natural in cylindrical. Skip it and you're working harder than you need to.
